title: What Does My Body Want Me to Know Today?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for listening to the body rather than pushing past it
excerpt: A journal prompt set for the habit of overriding physical signals. Asks the body directly what it is communicating, and what responding to that signal would actually require.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 1 body-listening prompt in WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use on any morning when you are about to push through something the body is signalling. Or as a daily check-in practice.

content.text: A journal prompt set for the habit of treating the body's signals as problems to manage rather than information to read. These prompts ask what the body is saying before you decide what to do about it.
How does my body feel right now, today?
Not how you think it should feel. What it actually feels like at this moment. Write a short, direct description.
Where in my body is there the most sensation right now?
Name the location. What kind of sensation is it?
If this sensation could speak, what would it say?
Write it in first person. Let the sensation tell you what it needs.
Is there something the body has been asking for that I have been putting off?
Rest. Movement. Food. Warmth. Fresh air. Quiet. Name it.
What is one small thing I could give the body today in response to what it told me?
Not a full lifestyle change. One thing, today.

Adapted from the Day 1 body-listening prompt in WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which opens with the principle that change begins from listening to where you are, not from requiring yourself to be somewhere else.
